pr.title: chore: add SECURITY.md with private reporting channel
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds a security policy declaring how to report vulnerabilities privately, plus explicit in-scope / out-of-scope language tied to Athanor's actual attack surface.

## Motivation

Athanor dispatches shell commands through Claude Code hooks, skills, and the Codex CLI. Concrete attack surfaces a finder might encounter:

- Hook prompts that execute shell commands beyond what the user authorized
- Skills writing outside the project root
- Codex CLI dispatch paths allowing argument injection (already partly addressed in PR #11)
- `.athanor/sessions/` or `.athanor/lessons/` artifacts leaking credentials

With no `SECURITY.md`, the default finder behavior is to open a public issue with the vulnerability inline — the wrong channel.

## Change

Single file: `SECURITY.md`. Codex CLI cross-review applied before submission (fallback-channel heading corrected, "documented sandbox" claim removed as unsupported, "released version on main" release/branch conflation fixed).

## Operational note

This assumes **GitHub private vulnerability reporting is enabled** for the repo:
*Settings > Security > Private vulnerability reporting > Enable*

If you prefer not to enable it, the fallback channel (public `security: contact request` issue → private channel) still works and is documented.

## Test plan

- [ ] `SECURITY.md` is discoverable via the GitHub Security tab
- [ ] Private advisory link resolves to a "Report a vulnerability" page (if private reporting enabled)
- [ ] Fallback issue title pattern is reasonable for a first-time finder

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr.author: Wooklae-cho
